explanation: The property states that whenever the 'Start-up Mode' is initiated (i.e., transitions from being false to being true), then in the next state, it must eventually be the case that all specified initialization actions are completed. These actions include loading parameters ('loadLog'), checking communication with sensors ('checkCommsSensors'), checking communication with valves ('checkCommsValves'), and checking communication with the GUI ('checkCommsGUI'). Additionally, the property ensures that if the 'Start-up Mode' is already active at the initial time, these same initialization actions must also eventually occur. The natural language phrase 'shall go through an initialization process that loads default parameters and checks...' is captured by the conjunction of these boolean expressions, implying that all these individual checks and loads are part of the process that eventually completes. The 'system memory' check mentioned in the natural language is not explicitly represented due to the lack of a specific atomic proposition for it.

The natural language statement specifies a response property: 'Upon initiation of X, Y shall happen'. This implies that when a particular event (initiation of Start-up Mode) occurs, a specific state (initialization being done, including loading parameters and checking communications) must eventually be reached. Template 69 is chosen because its first conjunct, `G (((! bool_exp1) & (X bool_exp1)) -> (X (F bool_exp3)))`, precisely captures the 'upon initiation' aspect by detecting the rising edge of `bool_exp1` (`(! bool_exp1) & (X bool_exp1)`) and then asserting that `bool_exp3` will eventually (`F`) be true in the future (`X`). The second conjunct, `(bool_exp1 -> (F bool_exp3))`, ensures this property holds even if `bool_exp1` is true at the very beginning of the system's execution. 

- `bool_exp1` is set to `StartUpMode` because it represents the 'initiation of the Start-up Mode'.
- `bool_exp3` is set to `initDone` because its definition ('indicates the configurations have been loaded and the internal checking is terminated') directly encompasses 'loads default parameters and checks the system memory and the communication of the controller with the sensors and valves, as well as between the controller and the GUI'.
- `bool_exp2` and `bool_exp4` are not used in this template and are set to 'True'.
- `N_DURATION` is null as the property uses an unbounded 'F' (eventually) operator.

The property states that globally, whenever the system/controller initialization starts (initStart), it is eventually the case that the default parameters are loaded (loadLog), the communication with the sensors is checked (checkCommsSensors), the communication with the valves is checked (checkCommsValves), and the communication with the GUI is checked (checkCommsGUI). This captures the requirement that upon initiation of the Start-up Mode, the system 'shall go through' this specified initialization process by ensuring these conditions are eventually met.

The property states that globally, if the 'Start-up Mode' is initiated (represented by 'bool_exp1' becoming true from false), then in the very next state, it must eventually be the case within a duration of N_DURATION steps that the specified initialization actions ('bool_exp3') are completed. The specified initialization actions include loading default parameters and checking communication with sensors, valves, and the GUI. The second part of the conjunction ensures that as long as the system is in 'Start-up Mode', these initialization actions eventually occur within the specified duration. This captures the requirement that upon entering the mode, the initialization process, comprising these specific checks and loads, must take place.

The property states that globally, whenever the 'Start-up Mode' is initiated (represented by 'StartUpMode' being true), it must eventually lead to the initialization process being completed (represented by 'initDone' being true). The 'initDone' atomic proposition describes the termination of the internal checking and loading of configurations, which encompasses the detailed initialization steps mentioned in the natural language (loading parameters, checking memory, and communications with sensors, valves, and GUI).

The property describes that upon the initiation of the Start-up Mode, the system will eventually complete its initialization process. 'Upon initiation of Start-up Mode' is captured by the LTL construct `(!StartUpMode & X StartUpMode)`, representing a rising edge or activation of `StartUpMode`. The phrase 'the system shall go through an initialization process' culminating in `initDone` (which indicates configurations loaded and internal checking terminated) is captured by `F initDone`. Template 29 is chosen because it precisely captures an 'on initiation' (rising edge) response property, ensuring that when `StartUpMode` is initiated (or is already active at the start), `initDone` will eventually occur.

The LTL property states that globally, whenever the system is in 'Start-up Mode' (bool_exp1), it must eventually reach a state where the initialization process is completed (bool_exp3). This captures the requirement that 'Upon initiation of the Start-up Mode, the system shall go through an initialization process that loads default parameters and checks the system memory and the communication of the controller with the sensors and valves, as well as between the controller and the GUI', implying that being in the Start-up Mode guarantees the eventual completion of the initialization tasks.

The property states that upon the initiation of the 'Start-up Mode' (represented by 'StartUpMode' becoming true), the system's initialization process must eventually be completed (represented by 'initDone'). The first part of the LTL formula, 'G (((! StartUpMode) & (X StartUpMode)) -> (X (F initDone)))', captures the 'upon initiation' aspect: if 'StartUpMode' was false in the current state and becomes true in the next state, then from that next state, 'initDone' must eventually become true. The second part, '(StartUpMode -> (F initDone))', handles the initial condition, ensuring that if 'StartUpMode' is already active at the very beginning of the system's execution, 'initDone' must still eventually be reached. The atomic proposition 'initDone' is chosen for 'bool_exp3' because its description 'indicates the configurations have been loaded and the internal checking is terminated' accurately encompasses all the specified steps of the initialization process (loading parameters, checking system memory, and checking communication with sensors, valves, and GUI).

The property states that whenever 'Start-up Mode' is initiated (i.e., it transitions from being false to true), then 'initDone' must eventually become true in the future. It also covers the case where 'Start-up Mode' is already active at the very beginning of observation, ensuring 'initDone' eventually holds. This directly captures the requirement that upon entering the 'Start-up Mode', the described initialization process (which concludes with 'initDone') shall occur.

The property states that whenever the 'Start-up Mode' is initiated (i.e., 'StartUpMode' transitions from false to true), then eventually, all specified initialization steps must be completed. This includes the system becoming 'initDone' (configurations loaded and internal checking terminated), parameters being loaded ('loadLog'), and communication checks with sensors ('checkCommsSensors'), valves ('checkCommsValves'), and the GUI ('checkCommsGUI') being successfully performed. The second part of the conjunction ensures that if 'StartUpMode' is already true at the beginning of observation, these conditions must also eventually hold.
